FILES IN THIS PACKAGE
================================================================================

1. README-accessibility-research.md
   → START HERE
   → Overview, scenarios, quick navigation guide
   → Best for: Understanding what's available

2. ada-accessibility-lawsuit-research-2026.md
   → MAIN FINDINGS
   → Complete data from 5 sources
   → Best for: Deep research, legal risk assessment

3. accessibility-data-sources-access-guide.md
   → TECHNICAL REFERENCE
   → How to access each data source
   → Machine-readable format analysis
   → Best for: Developers, data engineers

4. accessibility-data-collection-scripts.md
   → IMPLEMENTATION
   → Ready-to-use Python code
   → Best for: Building automated collectors

5. QUICK_START.txt (this file)
   → Navigation guide
   → Common questions answered

================================================================================
KEY STATISTICS AT A GLANCE
================================================================================

LAWSUITS:
  • 5,000+ ADA web lawsuits filed through 2025
  • 1,427 are repeat defendants (29% of cases)
  • New York: 33%+ of state lawsuits
  • E-commerce: 70% of all lawsuits

COMPLIANCE:
  • 94.8% of top 1M websites have WCAG failures
  • Only 5.2% have zero detected errors
  • Average: 51 errors per home page
  • Improvement: 10.3% from previous year

COMMON PROBLEMS (96% of all errors):
  1. Low contrast text: 79.1% of pages
  2. Missing alt text: 55.5% of pages
  3. Missing form labels: 48.2% of pages
  4. Empty links: 45.4% of pages

TECHNOLOGY IMPACT:
  • Best CMS: Divi (27.6 errors/page)
  • Worst CMS: 1C-Bitrix (97.0 errors/page)
  • Best Framework: AngularJS (37.7 errors/page)
  • Worst Framework: Mustache (89.1 errors/page)

================================================================================
I NEED TO...
================================================================================

Assess my lawsuit risk
  → Read: ada-accessibility-lawsuit-research-2026.md § Industry Concentration
  → Action: Check if your industry/company size in risk tables
  → Result: Understand exposure level

Understand compliance expectations
  → Read: ada-accessibility-lawsuit-research-2026.md § WebAIM Million Analysis
  → Action: Check what's "average" for your industry category
  → Result: Baseline for remediation planning

Build automated data collection
  → Read: accessibility-data-sources-access-guide.md
  → Copy: Code from accessibility-data-collection-scripts.md
  → Run: Master collector script
  → Result: Fresh data weekly/monthly

Check if my tech stack matters
  → Read: ada-accessibility-lawsuit-research-2026.md § Technology Impact
  → Search: Your CMS, JS framework, libraries
  → Compare: Error rates in your choices vs alternatives
  → Result: Tech debt to prioritize

Find data for a report/presentation
  → Read: ada-accessibility-lawsuit-research-2026.md
  → Use: Statistics from tables & findings
  → Reference: Original sources (UsableNet, WebAIM, Section 508, W3C)
  → Result: Credible data for stakeholders

Set up federal compliance monitoring
  → Read: accessibility-data-sources-access-guide.md § Section 508 Data
  → Download: Latest reports from section508.gov/manage/pmr/
  → Parse: Using section508_parser.py
  → Result: Agency baseline data

================================================================================
DATA SOURCES & DIRECT LINKS
================================================================================

WebAIM Million (Compliance Data)
  URL: https://webaim.org/projects/million/
  What: 1M website accessibility scans (Feb 2025)
  Format: HTML + embedded tables
  How to access: Scrape with provided script

UsableNet Lawsuit Reports (Legal Data)
  URL: https://blog.usablenet.com/ada-web-lawsuit-trends-2026
  What: 5,000+ lawsuits analyzed (2025 data, Jan 2026 report)
  Format: PDF report + blog article
  How to access: Download PDF or parse blog HTML

Section 508 Reports (Federal Compliance)
  URL: https://www.section508.gov/manage/reporting/
  What: Federal agency compliance metrics (archived PMRs)
  Format: PDF reports (Executive + Supplemental)
  How to access: Parse with provided PDF script

PACER Federal Courts (Real-time Lawsuits)
  URL: https://www.pacer.gov/
  What: All federal court filings (searchable)
  Format: Structured case data
  Cost: ~$0.10/page
  How to access: Use provided PACER client script

CourtListener (Free PACER Alternative)
  URL: https://www.courtlistener.com/api/
  What: Federal cases (free alternative to PACER)
  Format: JSON API
  Cost: Free
  How to access: Use provided CourtListener script

W3C WCAG Specifications
  URL: https://www.w3.org/WAI/WCAG22/
  What: Accessibility standards & guidelines
  Format: HTML + GitHub structured data
  How to access: Clone GitHub repo or parse website

================================================================================
PYTHON SCRIPTS PROVIDED
================================================================================

Required: pip install -r requirements.txt

webaim_scraper.py
  Usage: python webaim_scraper.py
  Output: webaim_data.json, webaim_*.csv
  Time: 30-60 seconds

ada_lawsuit_fetcher.py
  Usage: python ada_lawsuit_fetcher.py
  Output: ada_accessibility_lawsuits.csv
  Time: 1-2 minutes

section508_parser.py
  Usage: python section508_parser.py [pdf_file]
  Output: section508_agency_data.csv
  Time: 30 seconds per PDF

master_collector.py
  Usage: python master_collector.py
  Output: accessibility_data.json
  Time: 3-5 minutes (runs all above)

================================================================================
MOST IMPORTANT FINDINGS
================================================================================

1. WIDGET STRATEGIES DON'T WORK
   • Accessibility overlay widgets are ineffective
   • No meaningful reduction in lawsuits
   • Courts and plaintiffs expect substantive remediation
   • Recommendation: Focus on actual code-level fixes

2. REPEAT DEFENDANTS ARE COMMON
   • 29% of lawsuits target repeat defendants
   • 46% of federal cases are repeat defendants
   • Pattern: Settlement → minimal fix → new plaintiff → refiling
   • Recommendation: Comprehensive remediation on first fix

3. TECHNOLOGY CHOICES MATTER
   • Some frameworks correlate with 3.6x more errors
   • CMS choice: 27-97 errors/page variance
   • Best: React, Next.js, AngularJS
   • Worst: Mustache, Firebase, Redux libraries
   • Recommendation: Evaluate tech stack for accessibility

4. GEOGRAPHIC RISK VARIES DRAMATICALLY
   • New York = high risk (physical presence not required)
   • California = high risk
   • Florida = emerging high risk
   • Federal courts = 3,100+ cases nationwide
   • Recommendation: Assume nationwide exposure

5. LOW CONTRAST TEXT IS #1 PROBLEM
   • 79.1% of pages have low contrast issues
   • 96% of errors are 6 common types
   • Fixing top 6 error types would dramatically improve web
   • Recommendation: Low-hanging fruit for compliance

================================================================================
COMMON QUESTIONS
================================================================================

Q: Is my website at risk of ADA lawsuit?
A: Read industry section in main report. If e-commerce = ~70% risk
   If >$25M revenue = 36%+ of defendants. Geographic (NY/CA highest).
   Assume nationwide exposure.

Q: How do I reduce legal risk?
A: 1) Fix code-level barriers (not widgets)
   2) Comprehensive remediation (not surface fixes)
   3) Regular testing (automated + manual)
   4) Document efforts (creates liability defense)

Q: What's the easiest accessibility win?
A: Fix low contrast text (79% of pages have it).
   It's technically simple but visually obvious.

Q: Do accessibility overlay widgets work?
A: No. They're referenced in complaints as INEFFECTIVE.
   Focus on actual code fixes.

Q: Which technology stack should I choose?
A: Best: React (42.4 errors), Next.js (38.6), AngularJS (37.7)
   Avoid: Mustache (89.1), Firebase (74.6), Redux (78.2)

Q: How current is this data?
A: WebAIM: February 2025
   UsableNet: January 2026 (2025 data)
   Section 508: Spring 2023 (latest available)
   WCAG: Continuous updates
   Lawsuit data: Real-time via PACER (refresh monthly)

Q: Can I use these scripts in production?
A: Yes, they're designed for automated collection.
   Recommendation: Cache results to avoid duplicate requests.
   Consider rate limiting (1-2 second delays minimum).

Q: Where do I get more detailed information?
A: Read: ada-accessibility-lawsuit-research-2026.md (complete analysis)
   Reference: Original sources (WebAIM, UsableNet, etc.)
   Contact: Respective organizations for clarifications

================================================================================
GETTING STARTED (5 MINUTES)
================================================================================

Step 1: Read the summary (2 min)
  cat README-accessibility-research.md | head -100

Step 2: Check key findings (2 min)
  grep -A3 "CRITICAL" ada-accessibility-lawsuit-research-2026.md

Step 3: Determine next steps (1 min)
  → Legal risk assessment? → Industry section of main report
  → Data collection? → Run master_collector.py
  → Technical deep dive? → accessibility-data-collection-scripts.md

  less README-*       # Start reading

Quick reference:
  grep "KEY:" * | head -20    # Find important sections
  wc -l *                     # File sizes (words)

SUPPORT
================================================================================

Questions about the data?
  → Check: ada-accessibility-lawsuit-research-2026.md
  → Source: Listed in each section

Questions about accessing data programmatically?
  → Check: accessibility-data-sources-access-guide.md
  → See: accessibility-data-collection-scripts.md for examples

Script not working?
  → Check: requirements.txt (pip install -r requirements.txt)
  → Review: Error messages in script output
  → Verify: Internet connectivity for web sources

More resources:
  • WebAIM: webaim.org
  • W3C WCAG: w3.org/WAI/WCAG22/
  • Section 508: section508.gov
  • PACER: pacer.uscourts.gov
  • CourtListener: courtlistener.com
